title: Calm Is My New Home Base
subtitle: An affirmation for establishing peace as the default state rather than the exception
excerpt: A brief affirmation from Day 28 of a 30-day sleep programme. The statement names calm as a trained home base, not a lucky coincidence or a temporary reprieve, but the condition the nervous system is learning to return to automatically.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 28 theme of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795).

content.text: A brief affirmation from Day 28 of a 30-day sleep programme. Day 28 theme: trust rest as stability. The statement names calm as the baseline the nervous system is learning to hold, not a state you have to achieve each time from scratch, but the place it returns to more easily with each practice.
Say it three times, morning or evening. The second line is the one that distinguishes it from a wish: 'I return' rather than 'I will be calm'.
Calm is my new home base. I return to it more easily each day. My body knows this place and trusts it.
Adapted from the Day 28 theme of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
